acceptanceCriteria: Given a customer hardware need exists, When the product manager submits a new demand, Then a demand row is created with status NEW
Given the BOM is frozen at DS1, When the planner defines the build quantity, Then a build_plan v1 with status DRAFT and N builds with status PLANNED are created
Given builds exist, When the supply planner specifies material demand, Then build_demand rows and draft purchase_orders are created
Given a purchase order confirmed_eta is later than a build planned_start, When the simulator detects the shortage, Then the build material_status becomes AT_RISK
Given builds exist, When the planner sets priority, Then build.priority is updated

Given an ETA change, ER approval, priority change, or site change occurred, When the planner triggers a replan, Then a new build_plan version with status DRAFT is created and the previous version is archived
Given DS2 is frozen and engineering_release is APPROVED and a work_order is CREATED, When the CM locks the latest build_plan, Then build_plan.status becomes LOCKED and locked_at is set
Given the build_plan is LOCKED, When the HWM releases the build to a site, Then build.site_id is set, build.status becomes RELEASED, and build_plan.status becomes RELEASED
Given every build_demand for a build has qty_available greater than or equal to qty_required, When the simulator detects completion, Then build.material_status becomes KIT_READY
Given the line is BOOKED and material is KIT_READY, When production begins, Then line_booking.status becomes RUNNING and build.status becomes IN_PROGRESS with actual_start set

Given FAI passed and qty produced, When the build reaches RTD, Then build.status becomes RTD with actual_end set, line_booking.status becomes DONE, work_order.status becomes CLOSED, and N unit rows with status BUILT are created

ListDemands.description: Browse all demands with their lifecycle status and simulator progress. Powers the multi-demand dashboard view.

fields.name: progress
fields.description: Count of distinct domain events fired for this demand across the 28-event workflow (0-28).

fields.name: lastEventRef
fields.description: $ref of the most recent domain event recorded for this demand, or null if no events have fired yet.

fields.name: lastEventAt
fields.description: Timestamp of the most recent domain event recorded for this demand, or null if no events have fired yet.

GetDemand.description: Read the demand that this project will be linked to.

GetDemandWithBOMStatus.description: Demand details enriched with whether its project BOM is frozen at DS1, used before defining build quantity.

fields.name: projectStatus
fields.description: Latest design-state of the project's BOM (DRAFT, DS1, DS2_PROD).

GetBuildWithBOM.description: Build context together with its project's BOM lines, used to compute material requirements.

fields.name: bomItems
fields.description: BOM lines pulled from the parent project of this build.

ListBuildsAtRisk.description: Builds whose confirmed PO ETA is later than the build's planned_start. Computed by the simulator after every ETA change.

fields.name: latestPoEta
fields.description: Maximum confirmed_eta across the build's purchase orders.

ListBuilds.description: Builds visible to the planner for prioritization.

GetBuildPlanDisruptions.description: Current plan together with the disruption signals that justify a replan.

fields.name: etaSlips
fields.description: POs whose confirmed_eta moved later than the relevant build's planned_start.

fields.name: approvedEngineeringChanges
fields.description: ECs approved since the current plan version was created.

GetBuildPlanLockReadiness.description: Pre-flight check for the Lock gate: DS2 frozen, engineering release approved, work order created.

fields.name: ds2Frozen
fields.description: True when all bom_items design_state is DS2_PROD.

fields.name: engineeringReleaseApproved
fields.description: True when the project's engineering_release is APPROVED.

fields.name: workOrderCreated
fields.description: True when a work_order exists for this plan.

GetBuildMaterialStatus.description: Build with its build_demand rollup; the simulator checks whether qty_available >= qty_required for every part.

fields.name: isKitComplete
fields.description: True when every build_demand has qty_available >= qty_required.

GetBuildProductionReadiness.description: Build context with line booking and material readiness; production may only start when both gates are met.

fields.name: lineBookingStatus
fields.description: Latest line_booking.status for this build (BOOKED, RUNNING, DONE).

ListBuildsInProduction.description: Builds currently IN_PROGRESS, awaiting board test.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Filter on build.status (IN_PROGRESS).

ListBuildsReadyForFAI.description: Builds whose board tests have passed and are ready for First Article Inspection.

fields.name: boardTestsPassed
fields.description: True when every produced unit has a BOARD test_result with result PASS.

GetBuildTestStatus.description: Build with rollup of board test and FAI outcomes, used to confirm RTD readiness.

fields.name: faiPassed
fields.description: True when a FAI test_result with PASS exists.

ListBuildsReadyForPack.description: Builds at RTD with their associated units, awaiting pick and pack.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Filter on build.status (RTD).

Demand.description: A customer hardware order placed at the start of the flow; tracks the request from NEW through DELIVERED.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the demand

fields.name: customerId
fields.description: Customer who placed the demand

fields.name: productName
fields.description: Product/variant the customer is ordering

fields.name: qty
fields.description: Number of units requested

fields.name: requestedWeek
fields.description: ISO week the customer wants the units delivered

fields.name: status
fields.description: Demand lifecycle status (NEW, PLANNED, DELIVERED)

BomItem.description: A single line of the Bill of Materials within a project, with its design-state lifecycle.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the BOM item

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project that owns this BOM line

fields.name: partNumber
fields.description: Part number for this BOM line

fields.name: qtyPerUnit
fields.description: Quantity of this part required per produced unit

fields.name: designState
fields.description: Design state of the BOM line (DRAFT, DS1, DS2_PROD)

fields.name: frozenAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the BOM line was frozen at its current design state

BuildPlan.description: Versioned plan of builds for a demand; walks DRAFT → LOCKED → RELEASED.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the build plan

fields.name: demandId
fields.description: Demand this build plan was created for

fields.name: version
fields.description: Plan version, incremented on every Build Plan Updated event

fields.name: status
fields.description: Plan lifecycle status (DRAFT, LOCKED, RELEASED, SUPERSEDED). SUPERSEDED is set on the prior version when Update Build Plan creates a new one — preserving history while keeping a single current plan per demand.

fields.name: lockedAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the plan transitioned to LOCKED

fields.name: releasedAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the plan transitioned to RELEASED

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Trigger that caused the latest plan version (ETA_CHANGED, ER_APPROVED, PRIORITY_CHANGED, SITE_CHANGED)

fields.name: builds
fields.description: Builds scheduled under this plan

Build.description: A planned production batch under a build plan; tracks material readiness and production status.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the build

fields.name: buildPlanId
fields.description: Build plan that owns this build

fields.name: buildNo
fields.description: Human-readable sequence number for this build inside the plan

fields.name: qty
fields.description: Number of units this build produces

fields.name: priority
fields.description: Build priority used for sequencing

fields.name: siteId
fields.description: Production site the build is released to

fields.name: materialStatus
fields.description: Material readiness status (OPEN, AT_RISK, KIT_READY)

fields.name: plannedStart
fields.description: Planned production start date

fields.name: actualStart
fields.description: Actual production start timestamp

fields.name: actualEnd
fields.description: Actual production end timestamp (RTD)

fields.name: status
fields.description: Build lifecycle status (PLANNED, RELEASED, IN_PROGRESS, RTD, SHIPPED)

fields.name: buildDemand
fields.description: Material requirements per part for this build

fields.name: units
fields.description: Units produced by this build

BuildDemand.description: Per-build, per-part material requirement; required vs available quantity.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the build demand line

fields.name: buildId
fields.description: Build this material line belongs to

fields.name: partNumber
fields.description: Part number required for this build

fields.name: qtyRequired
fields.description: Quantity of the part required to complete the build

fields.name: qtyAvailable
fields.description: Quantity of the part currently received and available

ProductionSite.description: Manufacturing site where builds are produced.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the production site

fields.name: name
fields.description: Human-readable site name

Unit.description: An individual hardware unit produced from a build; tracked through pack and shipment.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the unit

fields.name: buildId
fields.description: Build that produced this unit

fields.name: serialNo
fields.description: Unit serial number

fields.name: status
fields.description: Unit lifecycle status (BUILT, PICKED, PACKED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, LOST)

fields.name: shipmentId
fields.description: Shipment this unit was packed into; null until Units Picked And Packed fires. Set atomically with the unit's PACKED status transition.

acceptanceCriteria: Given a demand exists, When the product manager creates a project, Then a project linked to the demand is created
Given a project exists, When the designer defines the BOM, Then bom_item rows are inserted with design_state DRAFT
Given a project has at least one bom_item, When the CM freezes the BOM at DS1, Then all bom_items design_state becomes DS1 and frozen_at is set
Given all open engineering changes are resolved, When the CM freezes the BOM at DS2, Then all bom_items design_state becomes DS2_PROD
Given DS2 is frozen, When the release authority approves the release, Then an engineering_release row with status APPROVED and approved_at is created

GetProject.description: Project that the BOM is being defined for.

GetProjectWithBOM.description: Project with its BOM lines, used before freezing the BOM at DS1.

GetBOMItem.description: BOM item the engineering change refers to.

GetProjectStatus.description: Project with gating signals: BOM design-state aggregate and count of open engineering changes.

fields.name: bomDesignState
fields.description: Most-restrictive design state across the BOM (DRAFT, DS1, DS2_PROD).

fields.name: openEngineeringChanges
fields.description: Count of engineering changes still OPEN for this project.

Project.description: PRIM project that owns the product structure and BOM for a demand.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the project

fields.name: demandId
fields.description: Demand this project was created for

fields.name: productName
fields.description: Product variant the project covers

fields.name: status
fields.description: Project lifecycle status

fields.name: bomItems
fields.description: Bill of Materials lines belonging to this project

fields.name: engineeringRelease
fields.description: Signed-off design package gating production for this project

BomItem.description: A single line of the Bill of Materials within a project, with its design-state lifecycle.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the BOM item

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project that owns this BOM line

fields.name: partNumber
fields.description: Part number for this BOM line

fields.name: qtyPerUnit
fields.description: Quantity of this part required per produced unit

fields.name: designState
fields.description: Design state of the BOM line (DRAFT, DS1, DS2_PROD)

fields.name: frozenAt
fields.description: Timestamp when the BOM line was frozen at its current design state

EngineeringRelease.description: Signed-off design package gating production lock; separate from the BOM freeze.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the engineering release

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project this release belongs to

fields.name: status
fields.description: Release status (OPEN, APPROVED)

fields.name: approvedAt
fields.description: Timestamp the release was approved

acceptanceCriteria: Given a purchase order is in DRAFT, When the buyer orders the material, Then the purchase order status becomes ORDERED and requested_date is set
Given a purchase order is ORDERED, When the supplier confirms, Then the purchase order status becomes CONFIRMED and confirmed_eta is set
Given a purchase order is CONFIRMED, When the supplier updates the ETA to a date strictly later than the current confirmed_eta, Then confirmed_eta is updated to the new value

Given a purchase order is CONFIRMED, When the supplier submits a new ETA that is equal to or earlier than the current confirmed_eta, Then the change is rejected with a domain error and confirmed_eta is unchanged
Given a PO is CONFIRMED and the ETA has been reached, When goods are received, Then purchase_order.status becomes RECEIVED, actual_receipt_date is set, and build_demand.qty_available is updated

fields.name: status
fields.description: Filter on PO status (DRAFT).

GetPurchaseOrder.description: Read a specific PO; reused across multiple PO lifecycle events.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Purchase order to retrieve

PurchaseOrder.description: Procurement order for a part from a supplier; walks DRAFT → ORDERED → CONFIRMED → RECEIVED.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the purchase order

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project this PO sources material for

fields.name: partNumber
fields.description: Part number being procured

fields.name: qty
fields.description: Quantity ordered

fields.name: supplierId
fields.description: Supplier providing the part

fields.name: requestedDate
fields.description: Date the buyer requested delivery on

fields.name: confirmedEta
fields.description: ETA confirmed by the supplier; may slip later

fields.name: actualReceiptDate
fields.description: Date goods were actually received

fields.name: status
fields.description: PO lifecycle status (DRAFT, ORDERED, CONFIRMED, RECEIVED)

WorkOrder.description: Production work order created as a precondition to Build Plan Lock.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the work order

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project this work order belongs to

fields.name: buildId
fields.description: Build the work order is for

fields.name: qty
fields.description: Quantity to be produced

fields.name: status
fields.description: Work order lifecycle status (CREATED, RELEASED, CLOSED)

acceptanceCriteria: Given a BOM is frozen at DS1, When a designer raises an engineering change, Then an engineering_change row is created with status OPEN
Given an engineering change is OPEN, When the CM approves it, Then the status becomes APPROVED and the affected bom_item is updated

ListOpenEngineeringChanges.description: Engineering changes still in OPEN status, awaiting approval.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Filter on EC status (OPEN).

EngineeringChange.description: A trouble report or change request against a BOM item, with OPEN/APPROVED/REJECTED lifecycle.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the engineering change

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project the engineering change belongs to

fields.name: bomItemId
fields.description: BOM line the change targets

fields.name: description
fields.description: What needs to change and why

fields.name: status
fields.description: Change lifecycle status (OPEN, APPROVED, REJECTED)

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: Timestamp the change was raised

fields.name: approvedAt
fields.description: Timestamp the change was approved

acceptanceCriteria: Given a build is RELEASED, When the production planner books a line, Then a line_booking row with status BOOKED is created

ListProductionLines.description: Production lines and their weekly capacity, filtered by site.

ProductionSite.description: Manufacturing site where builds are produced.

fields.name: id

fields.name: name
fields.description: Human-readable site name

ProductionLine.description: A production line within a site, with weekly capacity.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the production line

fields.name: siteId
fields.description: Production site this line belongs to

fields.name: name
fields.description: Line name

fields.name: capacityPerWeek
fields.description: Weekly production capacity in units

LineBooking.description: Reservation of a production line for a specific build.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the line booking

fields.name: lineId
fields.description: Production line reserved

fields.name: buildId
fields.description: Build the line is booked for

fields.name: plannedStart
fields.description: Planned start of the line booking

fields.name: plannedEnd
fields.description: Planned end of the line booking

fields.name: status
fields.description: Booking lifecycle status (BOOKED, RUNNING, DONE)

acceptanceCriteria: Given production is IN_PROGRESS, When board test passes, Then a test_result row with test_type BOARD and result PASS is created per produced unit
Given board tests have passed, When FAI passes, Then a test_result row with test_type FAI and result PASS is created

TestResult.description: Recorded outcome of a specific test type for a build or unit.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the test result

fields.name: buildId
fields.description: Build the test was executed on

fields.name: unitSerial
fields.description: Serial number of the unit tested

fields.name: testType
fields.description: Type of test (BOARD, FAI, FA2, FUNCTIONAL)

fields.name: result
fields.description: Test outcome (PASS, FAIL)

fields.name: executedAt
fields.description: Timestamp the test was executed

acceptanceCriteria: Given the build is RTD, When the warehouse picks and packs, Then a shipment row with status READY is created with packed_at set and unit.status becomes PACKED
Given the shipment is READY, When dispatched, Then shipment.status becomes IN_TRANSIT with shipped_at set, unit.status becomes SHIPPED, and build.status becomes SHIPPED
Given the shipment is IN_TRANSIT, When the customer receives it, Then shipment.status becomes DELIVERED with delivered_at set, unit.status becomes DELIVERED, and demand.status becomes DELIVERED

ListShipmentsReady.description: Shipments with status READY waiting for dispatch.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Filter on shipment.status (READY).

GetShipment.description: Current state of the shipment, used by the receiver to confirm delivery.

Shipment.description: Outbound shipment of one or more units to the customer.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the shipment

fields.name: demandId
fields.description: Demand the shipment fulfils

fields.name: packedAt
fields.description: Timestamp the units were packed

fields.name: shippedAt
fields.description: Timestamp the shipment was dispatched

fields.name: deliveredAt
fields.description: Timestamp the shipment was confirmed delivered

fields.name: status
fields.description: Shipment lifecycle status (PREPARING, READY, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED)

fields.name: units
fields.description: Units included in this shipment
